gold.rationale: The article builds a real event and real social-media posts into a more dramatic injury narrative than the available source basis supports. It also relies on an edited fight image and mismatched supporting visuals to make readers infer confirmed medical consequences and post-fight treatment that are not established by the cited source material.

atomic_points.post_summary: The post describes UFC Vegas 114, highlighting the debut victories of Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa, while noting that post-fight discussions focused on injury concerns, medical checks, and the violent nature of their performances.

sentence_level.sentence_text: LAS VEGAS — UFC Vegas 114 will be remembered as the night two newcomers, Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa, forced their way into the picture.
atoms: UFC Vegas 114 took place in Las Vegas.
Marwan Rahiki is a newcomer to UFC.
Hecher Sosa is a newcomer to UFC.
Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa gained attention at UFC Vegas 114.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Their victories put them on the map, but the reaction that followed gave the card a different tone by the end of the night.
atoms: Marwan Rahiki won his fight at UFC Vegas 114.
Hecher Sosa won his fight at UFC Vegas 114.
The reaction to the fights changed the tone of the event by the end of the night.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Social posts under #UFCVegas114 celebrated both debutants, yet much of the post-fight conversation had already shifted toward medical checks, injury concerns and the sense that both men had arrived in unusually violent circumstances.
atoms: Social media posts used the hashtag #UFCVegas114.
Social media posts celebrated Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa.
Post-fight conversation shifted toward medical checks.
Post-fight conversation shifted toward injury concerns.
The post claims both fighters arrived in unusually violent circumstances.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Rahiki made the more immediate impact.
atoms: Marwan Rahiki made a more immediate impact than Hecher Sosa.

sentence_level.sentence_text: His debut against Harry Hardwick carried the kind of intensity that spreads quickly online: forward pressure, heavy punches and a finish that drew instant attention across MMA social media.
atoms: Marwan Rahiki's debut opponent was Harry Hardwick.
Rahiki's fight against Hardwick involved forward pressure.
Rahiki's fight against Hardwick involved heavy punches.
The finish of Rahiki vs Hardwick drew instant attention on MMA social media.

sentence_level.sentence_text: UFC post-fight coverage and fan accounts pushed the idea that Rahiki had announced himself in emphatic fashion, and clips of the closing exchanges circulated rapidly as viewers replayed the damage from the second round.
atoms: UFC post-fight coverage portrayed Rahiki's performance as emphatic.
Fan accounts portrayed Rahiki's performance as emphatic.
Clips of the closing exchanges of Rahiki vs Hardwick circulated rapidly.
The damage in Rahiki vs Hardwick occurred in the second round.

sentence_level.sentence_text: What started as praise for a strong first showing soon took on a harsher edge.
atoms: Initial praise for Rahiki's performance took on a harsher edge.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Many watching described it as a jaw-breaking finish, the sort of stoppage that seems to tell its own story before any official wording catches up.
atoms: Viewers described Rahiki's finish as jaw-breaking.
The stoppage was described as telling its own story before official wording.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Rahiki’s late punching sequence became the defining image of his first UFC appearance, with post-fight stills and edited clips highlighting the punishment absorbed by Hardwick and reinforcing the feeling that this was not routine damage.
atoms: Rahiki's late punching sequence was the defining image of his UFC debut.
Post-fight stills highlighted the punishment absorbed by Harry Hardwick.
Edited clips highlighted the punishment absorbed by Harry Hardwick.
The damage to Hardwick was described as not routine.

sentence_level.sentence_text: By late in the evening, the broken-jaw finish had become one of the card’s central talking points.
atoms: The broken-jaw finish became a central talking point of UFC Vegas 114.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Hardwick could not continue, and the immediate visuals only strengthened the sense that Rahiki’s debut had crossed from impressive to troubling in a matter of moments.
atoms: Harry Hardwick could not continue the fight.
Visuals strengthened the sense that Rahiki's debut was troubling.

sentence_level.sentence_text: For those following in real time, it felt less like a standard TKO than an injury stoppage with immediate medical urgency, the kind of ending that can shape how a debut is remembered.
atoms: Observers felt the stoppage was an injury stoppage with immediate medical urgency.
Observers felt the stoppage was less like a standard TKO.

sentence_level.sentence_text: If Rahiki provided the card’s most brutal image, Sosa added another layer to the story.
atoms: Rahiki provided the card's most brutal image.
Hecher Sosa added another layer to the story of UFC Vegas 114.

sentence_level.sentence_text: He also won in his first UFC appearance and quickly drew celebratory attention, including upbeat posts from UFC Europe marking the result and his arrival on the roster.
atoms: Hecher Sosa won his first UFC appearance.
UFC Europe posted upbeat content about Hecher Sosa's result.
UFC Europe marked Sosa's arrival on the roster.

sentence_level.sentence_text: On social media, he was treated as another breakout newcomer from the same event.
atoms: Social media treated Hecher Sosa as a breakout newcomer.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The initial framing was simple enough: a winning debut, a composed performance and a meaningful first step.
atoms: The initial framing of Sosa's performance was a winning debut.
The initial framing of Sosa's performance was a composed performance.

sentence_level.sentence_text: As the night went on, that reading became less straightforward.
atoms: The reading of Sosa's performance became less straightforward as the night went on.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Even while celebratory images of Sosa continued to make the rounds, fight-night discussion moved past the scorecards and toward what some believed had happened after the bout.
atoms: Celebratory images of Sosa circulated.
Fight-night discussion moved toward events after the bout.

sentence_level.sentence_text: A late-night hospital-room image that circulated as part of the broader event fallout was folded into discussion of post-fight treatment, and among those tracking the card there was growing talk that Sosa, too, had needed hospital evaluation tied to damage sustained during the fight.
atoms: A late-night hospital-room image circulated regarding UFC Vegas 114.
The hospital-room image was folded into discussion of post-fight treatment.
There was growing talk that Hecher Sosa needed hospital evaluation.
The talk suggested Sosa's hospital evaluation was tied to damage sustained during the fight.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The win itself did not change, but the reaction around it did.
atoms: Hecher Sosa's win result did not change.
The reaction to Sosa's win changed.

sentence_level.sentence_text: On one side were the smiling event photos, fan applause and the standard coverage that follows a successful debut.
atoms: Smiling event photos were associated with Sosa's debut.
Fan applause was associated with Sosa's debut.

sentence_level.sentence_text: On the other was a rising sense that medical observation had become part of the story.
atoms: There was a rising sense that medical observation was part of Sosa's story.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The stream of images surrounding UFC Vegas 114 deepened that contrast, with celebration posts and injury-focused chatter colliding until Sosa’s night was being discussed not only as a successful debut but as part of a card that demanded extra attention once the cage action was over.
atoms: Images surrounding UFC Vegas 114 deepened the contrast between celebration and injury.
Sosa's night was discussed as part of a card demanding extra attention after cage action.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That mood shaped the way UFC Vegas 114 was being talked about across the sport by Sunday morning.
atoms: The mood shaped how UFC Vegas 114 was discussed by Sunday morning.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Observers pointed to it as another reminder of how quickly a debut showcase can become a conversation about matchmaking risk, especially when newcomers arrive with aggressive styles and immediate finishing intent.
atoms: Observers linked UFC Vegas 114 to conversations about matchmaking risk.
Observers noted newcomers arrived with aggressive styles.
Observers noted newcomers arrived with immediate finishing intent.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The strongest reactions were no longer confined to winners and losers.
atoms: The strongest reactions to UFC Vegas 114 were not confined to winners and losers.

sentence_level.sentence_text: They focused on what fans believed they had seen in the aftermath, which fighters might require further attention and whether late medical reviews would become a bigger issue coming out of the event.
atoms: Reactions focused on what fans believed they saw in the aftermath.
Reactions focused on which fighters might require further attention.
Reactions focused on whether late medical reviews would become a bigger issue.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The card also fed a familiar debate about how much fight-night damage is obvious in the moment and how much only becomes clear once the cameras are pointed elsewhere.
atoms: UFC Vegas 114 fed a debate about the visibility of fight-night damage.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Rahiki’s finish and the discussion around Sosa brought that issue to the forefront.
atoms: Rahiki's finish brought the issue of damage visibility to the forefront.
Discussion around Sosa brought the issue of damage visibility to the forefront.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In a few hours, an event built around action and new faces had become wrapped up in talk of fallout, medical checks and the uneasy line between a breakout performance and a dangerous one.
atoms: UFC Vegas 114 became wrapped up in talk of fallout.
UFC Vegas 114 became wrapped up in talk of medical checks.
UFC Vegas 114 became wrapped up in talk of the line between breakout and dangerous performance.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Social media helped cement that impression.
atoms: Social media helped cement the impression of UFC Vegas 114.

sentence_level.sentence_text: On X, posts using #UFCVegas114 amplified the sense that both newcomers had arrived, with Rahiki drawing the louder reaction because of the violence tied to his finish and Sosa earning broad support for a winning debut that still carried lingering concern afterward.
atoms: Posts on X used the hashtag #UFCVegas114.
Posts on X amplified the sense that both newcomers had arrived.
Rahiki drew a louder reaction on X due to violence tied to his finish.
Sosa earned broad support on X for a winning debut.
Sosa's debut carried lingering concern on X.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Fans embraced the imagery of statement performances, and that online momentum made the event feel larger than a standard Fight Night undercard breakthrough.
atoms: Fans embraced the imagery of statement performances.
Online momentum made UFC Vegas 114 feel larger than a standard Fight Night undercard breakthrough.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Betting chatter added its own strand to the aftermath, though it remained secondary to the debate over injuries and post-fight fallout.
atoms: Betting chatter was part of the aftermath of UFC Vegas 114.
Betting chatter remained secondary to the debate over injuries.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Recap posts from handicapping accounts celebrated profitable nights and broke down movement on the board, folding UFC Vegas 114 into the familiar language of units won, live plays and post-event analysis.
atoms: Handicapping accounts posted recaps of UFC Vegas 114.
Handicapping accounts celebrated profitable nights.
Handicapping accounts broke down movement on the board.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Even there, the conversation kept circling back to the same themes that defined the broader reaction: Rahiki’s destructive debut, Sosa’s successful arrival and the medical questions hanging over a card remembered as much for what followed the fights as for the finishes themselves.
atoms: Betting conversation circled back to Rahiki's destructive debut.
Betting conversation circled back to Sosa's successful arrival.
Betting conversation circled back to medical questions.
UFC Vegas 114 was remembered for what followed the fights as much as the finishes.

paragraph_level: UFC Vegas 114 featured debut victories for Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa, but the event's tone shifted from celebration to concern over injuries and medical checks.
Marwan Rahiki's debut against Harry Hardwick ended in a second-round stoppage described by viewers as a jaw-breaking injury stoppage rather than a standard TKO.
Visuals and social media posts highlighted the severe damage absorbed by Harry Hardwick, framing Rahiki's performance as unusually violent.
Hecher Sosa won his UFC debut and received initial celebratory attention, including from UFC Europe, but post-fight discussions raised concerns about his health.
A circulated image of a hospital room led to speculation that Hecher Sosa required hospital evaluation for damage sustained during his fight.
The event sparked broader debates about matchmaking risks, the visibility of fight-night damage, and the line between breakout performances and dangerous violence.
Social media on X amplified the narrative of both fighters' arrivals, with Rahiki drawing attention for violence and Sosa for a win with lingering medical concerns.
Betting discussions and handicapping recaps also reflected the dominant themes of Rahiki's destructive debut, Sosa's arrival, and post-fight medical questions.

Cage padding with 'MAYA GEBALA' and 'Aviator' branding is visible.

retrieved_evidence.text:
Vamos Hecher 🫡 🇪🇸 #UFCVegas114 

Successful debut for Hecher Sosa last night!

Hecher Sosa’s first outing under the UFC banner at the recent Vegas 114 card was broadly framed as a successful debut, with promotional accounts and regional outlets drawing attention to his performance. According to posts monitored over the weekend, UFC Europe’s official channels published congratulatory messages and highlight footage focusing on key moments from Sosa’s bout. The content, which included short edit clips and post-fight images, was shared by supporters and Spanish-speaking fan communities, reinforcing the impression that the newcomer had capitalised on his opportunity and strengthened his profile within the promotion’s European audience.

Hecher Sosa’s first appearance at UFC Vegas 114 has been highlighted as another major breakthrough, with accounts noting that he raised his arms in celebration after securing a successful debut win before concerns later emerged about injuries suffered during the bout. According to commentary from analysts, Sosa’s performance combined high output with a willingness to absorb damage in scrambles, contributing to the perception of a chaotic, injury-filled contest. Such post-fight victory celebrations inside the octagon are a routine part of UFC events, marking the moment a newcomer confirms a place on the roster even as medical staff begin more detailed checks backstage.

Came in breaking jaws😳

Yesterday Marwan Rahiki made a statement in his debut! #UFCVegas114

Analysts following the digital footprint of UFC Vegas 114 noted that online reaction helped elevate the profiles of debutants Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa beyond the confines of the live broadcast. According to fan forums and social platforms reviewed in recent days, highlight reels, reaction threads and congratulatory posts were repeatedly circulated, giving the impression that both fighters had effectively announced their arrival on the card. Commenters point out that this wave of attention, while not unusual after a major event, appeared particularly concentrated on the two newcomers, whose names featured prominently in trending discussions tied to the event hashtag.

Marwan Rahiki was widely portrayed by mixed martial arts commentators as making an immediate impact in his first appearance on a UFC stage at the Vegas 114 event, according to early coverage. Reports and social-media posts described him as relying on heavy striking combinations and forward pressure to secure a debut win that stood out on the preliminary portion of the card. In the aftermath, clips of Rahiki’s bout and still images showing key exchanges were repeatedly shared by fans, many of whom praised his aggressive style and suggested he could become a figure to watch if matched quickly in future contests.

Discussion of the UFC Vegas 114 event in combat-sports communities has continued well beyond the official results, with conversations ranging from technical breakdowns to the condition of athletes in the aftermath, according to posts compiled by fan analysts. On message boards and social platforms, users not only revisited the performances of debut winners such as Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa, but also exchanged views about medical updates, visible swelling and other details they believed they observed in post-fight photographs. Commentators say this broader focus reflects a regular pattern in which major cards prompt prolonged debate about fighter welfare and the physical toll of the contests.

Marwan Rahiki’s debut at UFC Vegas 114 has been described in recent reports as one of the card’s most violent turning points, with accounts focusing on a heavy punching exchange that allegedly left his opponent nursing a suspected broken jaw. According to observers cage-side, Rahiki pressed forward aggressively, using sharp boxing combinations and forward pressure to overwhelm a fellow newcomer. This kind of emphatic finish, especially when it appears to cause visible facial damage, often shapes how a UFC event is remembered and can immediately elevate a debuting athlete’s profile with matchmakers and fans alike.

Social media activity surrounding the recent UFC Vegas 114 fight card has drawn attention to posts highlighting debut victories by fighters Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa, according to observers tracking online engagement. In the hours following the event, short video clips, photographs and congratulatory messages were widely circulated on major platforms, with hashtags referencing the card gaining additional visibility. Commentators note that the volume of posts featuring the two newcomers, including images from the backstage area and brief celebratory messages, helped frame their first appearances in the promotion as notable storylines on a night otherwise dominated by more established names.

Post-event discussions of UFC Vegas 114 have also referenced a hospital setting, with some reports describing a fighter receiving medical attention after the physically taxing bouts on the card. According to commentators familiar with the promotion’s protocols, athletes such as Hecher Sosa may be transported for further scans or treatment if there are concerns about concussions, fractures or other acute injuries. Hospital rooms in this context typically feature monitoring equipment and basic trauma care, underscoring how the sport’s regulated environment extends beyond the octagon to include mandatory medical evaluations following especially violent or chaotic contests.

Alongside the sporting narratives that emerged from UFC Vegas 114, online betting discussions formed a noticeable secondary strand of conversation, according to monitoring of gambling forums and social feeds. Users exchanged comments on pre-fight odds, unexpected outcomes and how debut victories by fighters including Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa affected combination wagers and live-betting decisions. While this chatter contributed to the wider event ecosystem, analysts emphasised that it tended to sit in the background of broader coverage, which primarily concentrated on in-cage performances, promotional reaction and the growing online visibility of the card’s newcomers.

UFC Vegas 114 generated substantial interest among online betting communities, where participants often rely on spreadsheet-style trackers to log wagers, odds and outcomes across an entire card. In recent discussions, such records have been associated with the bouts involving Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa, reflecting how debut fighters quickly become focal points for speculative betting once their style is partially known. These digital ledgers usually list fighter names, moneyline prices, prop bets and final results, providing an organized overview of how closely an event was followed and how strongly its violent finishes influenced both real-time betting decisions and post-fight analysis on social media.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post fabricates specific medical narratives (broken jaw, hospitalization) not supported by evidence and uses images from completely different events/fighters, creating a severe semantic mismatch.
text_analysis.t1_evidence: The post claims Rahiki delivered a 'jaw-breaking finish' and that Sosa needed 'hospital evaluation' (sentences 8, 19), but the evidence only mentions 'breaking jaws' as a social media caption (src_422_1) and general 'medical updates/swelling' discussions (news_422_2, news_422_7), with no corroboration of a confirmed broken jaw or hospital admission.
t1_problematic_subclaims: Sosa needed hospital evaluation tied to damage sustained during the fight
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post frames the event as defined by 'medical urgency' and 'injury concerns' (sentence 3, 12), whereas the evidence describes the reaction as 'congratulatory messages', 'highlight footage', and 'regular pattern' of debate about fighter welfare (news_422_2, news_422_7), significantly inflating the severity and tone of the aftermath.
t2_problematic_subclaims: The reaction shifted toward medical checks and injury concerns
Rahiki's debut crossed from impressive to troubling
text_analysis.t3_evidence: The post presents image_1 as evidence of Rahiki's finish, but the image OCR identifies 'Maya Gebala', a different fighter; the post presents image_3 as Sosa in a hospital, but the image OCR shows 'JFC' and '@asplund', indicating a different event/source context.
t3_problematic_subclaims: Image 3 shows Sosa in a hospital room
text_analysis.c1_evidence: The post text claims image_1 depicts Marwan Rahiki's fight, but the image description and OCR identify the fighter as 'Maya Gebala'; the post claims image_3 depicts Hecher Sosa in a hospital, but the image entities include 'JFC' and '@asplund', contradicting the UFC Vegas 114/Sosa context.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is highly unreliable, combining fabricated medical details with misattributed imagery from other events to construct a dramatic narrative unsupported by the ground truth.

image_analysis.global_notes: All images are authentic visual evidence corresponding to the specific claims made in the post text and the ground-truth evidence.

judge.level3_rationale: The post is False because it fabricates specific injury details (broken jaw) and uses images from entirely different events and fighters (Maya Gebala, JFC) to falsely support its claims about Marwan Rahiki and Hecher Sosa, creating a misleading narrative with no factual basis for its core visual and medical assertions.
judge.reasoning_trace: The post claims Rahiki broke Hardwick's jaw and shows an image of Maya Gebala as proof; it claims Sosa was hospitalized and shows an image of a JFC fighter as proof. These are clear T1 (fabrication of injury specifics not in evidence) and V2/C1/C2 (wrong images). With 6 L2 labels and T1 present, the verdict is False.
judge.core_claim: UFC Vegas 114 was defined by the violent debut of Marwan Rahiki, who broke Harry Hardwick's jaw, and Hecher Sosa, who required hospital evaluation after his win.
key_subclaims: Marwan Rahiki defeated Harry Hardwick in a 'jaw-breaking' finish at UFC Vegas 114.
Hecher Sosa won his UFC debut at UFC Vegas 114.
Hecher Sosa required hospital evaluation for damage sustained during his fight.
Image 1 depicts Marwan Rahiki's fight against Harry Hardwick.
Image 3 depicts Hecher Sosa in a hospital room after his fight.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Marwan Rahiki defeated Harry Hardwick in a 'jaw-breaking' finish at UFC Vegas 114.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms Rahiki made an impact with heavy striking, but does not confirm a 'broken jaw' or opponent 'Harry Hardwick'. The specific injury narrative is likely fabricated or exaggerated.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Hecher Sosa won his UFC debut at UFC Vegas 114.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms Sosa had a successful debut at UFC Vegas 114.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Hecher Sosa required hospital evaluation for damage sustained during his fight.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence mentions hospital settings generally for fighters, but does not confirm Sosa specifically needed hospital evaluation for fight damage.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Image 1 depicts Marwan Rahiki's fight against Harry Hardwick.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 1 clearly shows 'MAYA GEBALA' on the cage post, indicating it is from a different event/fighter than Rahiki's debut.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Image 3 depicts Hecher Sosa in a hospital room after his fight.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 3 shows a fighter with 'JFC' on his shirt and an Instagram handle '@asplund', which does not match Hecher Sosa (UFC fighter). This is an image from a different event/fighter.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post contains a mix of true elements (Sosa's win) and significant fabrications/misleading elements. Specifically, it fabricates a 'broken jaw' narrative for Rahiki and misidentifies images from completely different events (Maya Gebala, JFC fighter) as Rahiki and Sosa. This results in 6 L2 labels firing, including T1 (fabricated injury details), V2 (wrong images used as evidence), and C1/C2 (semantic/contextual mismatch between text and images). According to the rules, 6+ L2 labels with a fabrication label (T1) mandates a 'False' verdict.

image_findings.explanation: The image is from a different event (Maya Gebala) but is presented as evidence of Rahiki's fight, constituting visual editing/misframing.
The image shows a UFC fighter celebrating, consistent with the text's description of Sosa's victory.
The image shows a fighter with 'JFC' branding and '@asplund' handle, not Hecher Sosa, yet is presented as Sosa in the hospital.
The image is a betting spreadsheet, consistent with the text's mention of betting chatter.
